findings.title: Public EmbeddedConfig sampler setters have no direct regression coverage
findings.impact: The PR replaces the public embedded-worker LogRateLimit API with RequestSampleRate, AccessSampleRate, and DiagnosticSampleRate, but the added tests exercise LogSampler internals rather than this public path. A typo in the extraArgs key, bad string conversion, or invalid-rate handling would leave embedded workers running the wrong sampling policy without failing any test.
findings.fix: Add an EmbeddedConfig-focused unit test that calls all three setters, verifies the emitted extraArgs names and values, verifies chaining, and covers invalid, NaN, and out-of-range inputs leaving the prior config unchanged.

findings.title: Cross-process sampling test does not assert the operation or propagation outcome
findings.impact: CrossProcessSamplingConsistency claims to verify client-to-worker sampling consistency, but inside the workload it only calls Set/Get/Del and ignores failures and log/sample state. The test would still pass if Set failed for every operation, if Get/Del failed, or if the worker never received the propagated sampling decision.
findings.fix: Assert each client operation succeeds and add an observable propagation check, such as forcing deterministic admit/reject decisions and verifying the worker-side metadata/log sample state or emitted log behavior matches the client decision.
